objective: Recover the blocked Rust 1.80 representation-only correction through resolver-owned dependency closure and additively supersede the flawed v1 current projection with fail-closed historical-pin replay plus one durable forward-current checker, without changing any historical checker.
outputs: An additive v2 manifest binds all eighteen immutable historical gates to receipt-containing ancestral commits and exact checker/evidence hashes, and separately names one forward-current LSLC-003J checker.
The dispatcher validates canonical roles and replays historical gates only in bounded isolated clean materializations with verified cleanup, while current gates execute on the live tree.

The preserved three-literal Rust 1.80 correction closes through the exact descriptor hash, resolver-owned revision-13 lock, activation binding, compatibility fixtures, and workspace projection with unchanged bytes and behavior.
non_scope: Editing, weakening, deleting, bypassing, or relabeling any historical checker or the v1 manifest and LSLC-003H acceptance history.

Clippy policy/baseline or warning cleanup, broad documentation compaction, runtime/feature breadth, dependencies, devices, public API or protocol behavior, authority expansion, Manifold integration, or external source intake.

Live descriptor source hash, revision-13 resolver lock/fingerprint, activation binding, compatibility fixtures, module/workspace projection, canonical dispatcher ownership, CI/aggregate routing, and immutable historical hashes agree.
The exact three literals compile on pinned Rust 1.80 and focused/ordinary tests preserve initialization bytes and runtime behavior.
The complete fail-closed v2 dispatch, ordinary tests, public boundary, and owner invariants pass without historical edits, devices, dependencies, breadth, API, protocol, or authority change.
